user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Alpine Ski House.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 16:37 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House escalation triage

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 12:06 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 13:18 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-02-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Alpine Ski House)

[00:02:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:06:00] Priya (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:10:00] Priya (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Priya (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Priya (Alpine Ski House): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:22:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:24:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
